choice: Adopt (a)+(b)+(c). Persist an explicit external-account-id ↔ Account mapping (confirmed once, reused thereafter); auto-create flagged Holding stubs for unknown tickers so nothing is dropped; and on a full position sync, treat omitted holdings as zeroed-out but surface them for confirmation before depleting net worth. Manual entry and CSV reuse the same stub/flag path for unknown tickers.
rationale: Read-only sync and CSV both feed actuals that drive the Plan comparison, so a silent drop or a stale non-zero balance directly corrupts a core deliverable. An explicit account mapping avoids guessing which of several Accounts a brokerage account is; flagged stubs preserve data without auto-trusting it; and handling disappearance prevents a sold-out position from inflating net worth forever. This is the auth/error-edge of ingestion — down/partial/unknown inputs — that the area can't be groomed past without settling.
